hypothesis: Incorporating a dynamic, real-time adjustment of the bicarbonate-to-carbonate ratio during electrolysis—guided by an online surrogate model that continuously monitors instantaneous JCO and UCO2 signals—will maintain reactor yield above 40 mA cm⁻² across a wider range of inlet capture solution compositions than any single fixed electrolyte ratio can achieve, because the optimal bicarbonate:carbonate balance shifts predictably with current density and flow rate in a manner that a static operating point cannot track but a closed-loop adaptive controller can exploit.
